meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. An Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code for fast MVPs.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap kills SaaS momentum. To solve this, deploy a high-fidelity SaaS starter kit using automated scaffolding. By integrating Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative feature development, you can ship a production-ready MVP in hours, effectively demonstrating technical authority and securing your first paying customers immediately.
content.intro:
You have the vision, the drive, and the market insight, but you are staring at a blank repository. The 'no portfolio' syndrome is the silent killer of founder ambition; it’s the gap between a brilliant idea and the tangible proof required to win trust. Without a live product, you are just another dreamer in a sea of noise.

But the era of hand-coding boilerplate is dead. By leveraging modern SaaS starter kits, you don't just build a website—you engineer a business foundation. We are moving beyond simple prototypes into the realm of rapid, scalable architecture, allowing you to bypass the engineering drudgery and go straight to the business of solving problems for profit.

steps.title: Architecting the Foundation
steps.description: Establish the core stack using a robust boilerplate to ensure auth, payments, and database schemas are handled from minute one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 SaaS starter kit with Supabase authentication, Stripe integration, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure a modular folder structure that separates business logic from UI components.

steps.title: Implementing Data Models
steps.description: Define your schema using schema-first development to ensure your database is ready for real-world user traffic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a SaaS user profile, subscription status, and a 'projects' table. Include relationships for multi-tenant data isolation.

steps.title: Building the Core Feature
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on your core value proposition—the specific tool your customers will pay for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a server-side API route for the core feature that processes user input and returns a structured JSON response, including error handling and Zod validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Implement the core AI processing logic in route.ts with robust error handling'

steps.title: Integrating Stripe Payments
steps.description: Connect your MVP to the real world by setting up webhooks and checkout sessions to capture revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Stripe webhooks to handle subscription upgrades and cancellations, updating the user record in Supabase automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm install stripe && npx stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/webhooks'

steps.title: Deployment and Verification
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and run automated smoke tests to ensure your production environment is stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script and a set of Playwright test cases that verify the login flow and the primary feature output.

pro_tips: Always use Environment Variable management from day one; never hardcode keys, even in private repos.
Prioritize 'Stripe-first' development: if you can't charge for it, the feature isn't worth building yet.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation as you build; your future self and potential co-founders will thank you.
Keep your database schema lean; YAGNI (You Ain't Gonna Need It) applies to database columns just as much as code.
Automate your feedback loop by linking your production error logs directly to a dedicated Slack channel.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on acquiring your first 10 users through direct outreach rather than building more features. Validate the problem, collect revenue, and iterate based on real usage data.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'no portfolio' label define your potential. You have the tools, the blueprint, and the expertise to launch a profitable SaaS today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
